terms.definition: Rescaling numeric columns so magnitudes and spreads are comparable across features before modeling, without changing tree split order for monotone transforms.
sklearn scaler that standardizes each feature with z = (x − μ) / σ using training mean μ and sample standard deviation σ (ddof=1).
sklearn scaler that maps each feature linearly from training min–max into a target interval, default [0, 1]: x′ = (x − min) / (max − min) rescaled to feature_range.
Centering and dividing by spread to obtain zero-mean, unit-variance coordinates on the training set; colloquially implemented via StandardScaler.
In ML preprocessing, often means rescaling to a bounded range (MinMax) or to comparable scale; distinct from statistical normalization to a probability distribution.
Fitting scaler statistics on validation, test, or full-dataset rows before evaluation, letting holdout information influence preprocessing.
